description: MITRE ATT&CK ground truth anchors for HYDRA detection accuracy framework

primary_indicators: powershell.exe spawned with -EncodedCommand or -e flag
Base64-encoded command line arguments exceeding 500 characters
PowerShell script block logging events (EventID 4104) with suspicious keywords
Invocation of Net.WebClient DownloadString or DownloadFile methods
PowerShell execution policy bypass via -ExecutionPolicy Bypass flag
Login from previously unseen geographic location or IP range
Multiple cloud service authentications within short time window from different regions
Cloud API calls using service account credentials from non-infrastructure IP
Successful authentication after multiple failed attempts from different source IPs
Access token usage from IP address not matching token issuance location
Rapid file extension changes across multiple directories (e.g., .encrypted, .locked, .crypt)
High volume of file write operations with entropy increase in file contents
Deletion or modification of Volume Shadow Copies (vssadmin delete shadows)
Ransom note file creation (README.txt, DECRYPT_FILES.html) in multiple directories
Process accessing large number of files in quick succession with write operations
certutil.exe used with -urlcache or -split flags to download files
bitsadmin.exe /transfer commands downloading executables from external URLs
PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest or curl.exe downloading PE files to temp directories
Outbound HTTP/HTTPS connections from non-browser processes to uncommon domains
File creation in writable directories (Temp, AppData, ProgramData) following network connection
Process accessing lsass.exe memory with PROCESS_VM_READ permissions
Procdump.exe or comsvcs.dll MiniDump invocation targeting lsass.exe PID
Creation of .dmp files containing LSASS memory regions
Sysmon EventID 10 with TargetImage lsass.exe and GrantedAccess 0x1010 or 0x1FFFFF
Unsigned or untrusted process opening handle to lsass.exe
Web server process spawning unexpected child processes (cmd.exe, sh, bash)
HTTP requests containing known exploit payloads or SQLi/RCE patterns in URI or body
Anomalous POST request sizes or encoding to web application endpoints
Web application errors correlating with subsequent unauthorized file creation
WAF or IDS alerts for CVE-specific exploit signatures against public services
Multiple failed authentication attempts (EventID 4625) from single source IP exceeding threshold
Sequential or distributed login attempts against multiple user accounts
Authentication failures followed by a single success from the same source
High-frequency SSH or RDP connection attempts from external IP addresses
Account lockout events (EventID 4740) across multiple accounts in short window
Non-browser processes making persistent HTTP/HTTPS connections to external hosts
HTTP beaconing pattern with regular interval connections to single domain
Unusually large HTTP POST requests or encoded data in URL parameters from endpoint
HTTPS connections to recently registered or low-reputation domains
HTTP traffic with custom User-Agent strings or non-standard headers from endpoint processes
TLS connections to IP addresses without valid certificates or with self-signed certificates
Encrypted traffic on non-standard ports (not 443) from endpoint processes
High-entropy data transfer over established connections with regular beacon intervals
JA3/JA3S hash matches for known malware TLS fingerprints
DNS-over-HTTPS or DNS-over-TLS to non-corporate resolvers for command channel
RDP connections (port 3389) between internal hosts not in baseline communication pattern
EventID 4624 Logon Type 10 from unexpected source hosts
RDP session initiated by service account or non-interactive user
Multiple RDP sessions from single source to many destinations in short window
RDP connection following credential access activity on source host
Email with attachment from external sender containing macro-enabled Office document
Office process (WINWORD.EXE, EXCEL.EXE) spawning cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or wscript.exe
Macro execution (EventID 4116) followed by network connection from Office process
Email attachment with double extension (.pdf.exe) or known lure filename patterns
VBA macro invoking Shell, WScript.Shell, or CreateObject for process execution

anchors.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell
primary_indicators: cmd.exe spawned by unexpected parent process (Office apps, browser, Java)
Command line containing chained operators with reconnaissance commands (whoami, net user, ipconfig)
cmd.exe executing commands with obfuscation (caret insertion, environment variable substitution)
Batch file creation and immediate execution from temp or user-writable directories
cmd.exe /c with piped output redirected to file in staging directory
schtasks.exe /create invoked with command pointing to executable in temp or user directory
Scheduled task creation (EventID 4698) with SYSTEM-level privileges by non-admin user
Task registered to execute at logon or on a recurring schedule from non-standard path
XML task definition containing encoded PowerShell or download commands
Scheduled task created via COM object (Schedule.Service) from script engine process

anchors.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
primary_indicators: Service creation (EventID 7045) with binary path pointing to temp or user-writable directory
sc.exe create or New-Service cmdlet invoked from non-admin context or unusual parent
Service binary replaced or modified (file hash change) on existing legitimate service
Service configured to run as LocalSystem with binary in non-standard location
Registry modification under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services by non-installer process

anchors.technique_name: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
primary_indicators: EventID 1102 (audit log cleared) or 104 (system log cleared) in Security or System logs
wevtutil.exe cl invocation clearing Security, System, or PowerShell logs
Clear-EventLog PowerShell cmdlet execution targeting security-relevant log channels
Multiple event log channels cleared within short time window
Event log service stopped or disabled following suspicious activity

anchors.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
primary_indicators: Antivirus or EDR service stopped or disabled via sc.exe, net stop, or Set-MpPreference
Windows Defender exclusion paths added via PowerShell or registry modification
Tamper protection bypass attempts or security product uninstallation commands
AMSI bypass techniques (amsiInitFailed, reflection-based patching) in script block logs
Firewall rules modified or disabled (netsh advfirewall set allprofiles state off)

anchors.technique_name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
primary_indicators: Large outbound DNS TXT queries or high volume of DNS requests to single domain
FTP or TFTP connections to external hosts from endpoints not authorized for file transfer
ICMP packets with payload sizes exceeding normal ping traffic (data in echo requests)
Outbound SMTP connections from non-mail processes or to non-corporate mail servers
Unusual volume of data transferred over allowed protocols to external destinations
Outbound data volume spike on established C2 connection exceeding baseline
File read operations on sensitive directories followed by network transmission on same connection
Compressed or archived files created in staging directory prior to outbound transfer
Beaconing connection transitioning from small keepalive packets to large data uploads
Process reading credential stores or database files with subsequent network activity
Process calling DuplicateTokenEx or ImpersonateLoggedOnUser API functions
Token manipulation detected via Sysmon EventID 1 with elevated integrity from low-privilege parent
Process running as SYSTEM spawned from user-level parent without UAC elevation
Named pipe impersonation (ImpersonateNamedPipeClient) from non-service process
Security token privilege escalation (SeDebugPrivilege, SeImpersonatePrivilege) enabled at runtime

anchors.technique_name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting
primary_indicators: TGS requests (EventID 4769) for service accounts with RC4 encryption (0x17) from single source
High volume of TGS requests for multiple SPNs from single user or workstation
Kerberos ticket requests for service accounts followed by offline brute force indicators
Tools like Rubeus or Invoke-Kerberoast detected in command line or script block logs
Anomalous Kerberos traffic pattern: single host requesting tickets for many service accounts
Execution of net user, net localgroup, or wmic useraccount commands in sequence
PowerShell Get-LocalUser or Get-LocalGroupMember cmdlets from non-admin scripts
Multiple account enumeration commands executed within short time window
LDAP queries for user objects from non-domain-admin workstation
Process spawning reconnaissance tools (whoami /all, quser, query user) in chain
Single host initiating TCP SYN connections to multiple ports on single or multiple targets
Nmap, masscan, or similar scanning tool process execution detected on endpoint
High volume of connection attempts with RST responses indicating port scanning
Sequential port access pattern (incrementing port numbers) from single source
Internal host scanning RFC1918 address ranges on common service ports (22, 80, 443, 3389)
External IP performing sequential connection attempts against organization's public IP range
Firewall logs showing single source scanning multiple destination IPs on same port
IDS/IPS alerts for network sweep or host discovery from external IP
ICMP echo requests to sequential IP addresses from single external source
SYN packets to common ports across multiple public-facing IPs within short window
Login with default vendor credentials (admin/admin, root/root) on network devices or appliances
Authentication with built-in administrator account (RID 500) from external or unexpected source
Default service account (sa, postgres, tomcat) used for interactive logon
Guest account enabled and used for authentication (EventID 4624 with Guest SID)
Default credentials detected in HTTP Basic auth headers to management interfaces

anchors.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys
primary_indicators: Registry value created under Run/RunOnce keys pointing to executable in temp or user directory
Sysmon EventID 13 for registry modification on HKCU or HKLM autostart keys
Non-installer process writing to Startup folder or registry autostart locations
Registry Run key value containing encoded PowerShell or script interpreter invocation
Persistence established via registry key with value pointing to DLL loaded by rundll32

anchors.technique_name: Event Triggered Execution: WMI Event Subscription
primary_indicators: WMI permanent event subscription created (EventID 5861) with CommandLineEventConsumer
WMI event filter and consumer binding targeting system startup or user logon events
wmiprvse.exe spawning unexpected child processes (cmd.exe, powershell.exe)
MOF file compilation (mofcomp.exe) containing event subscription definitions
WMI subscription referencing script or executable in non-standard filesystem location
Process calling CreateRemoteThread in target process address space for DLL loading
VirtualAllocEx and WriteProcessMemory API sequence targeting remote process
Unsigned DLL loaded into legitimate system process from non-standard path
Sysmon EventID 8 (CreateRemoteThread) with source process in user-writable directory
Process hollowing indicators: NtUnmapViewOfSection followed by memory write and thread resume

anchors.technique_name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location
primary_indicators: Executable named as system binary (svchost.exe, csrss.exe) running from non-System32 path
Process with legitimate name but unsigned or with non-Microsoft digital signature
Binary with name matching Windows component but hash not in known-good baseline
Process spawned from user-writable directory with name matching system utility
File created with right-to-left override character in filename to disguise extension
Script files with high entropy content or extensive string concatenation obfuscation
PowerShell scripts using character array joining, base64, or XOR encoding for payload
Executable with packed or encrypted sections detected by entropy analysis
Multi-layer encoded command line arguments (base64 within base64, gzip+base64)
JavaScript or VBScript files with string reversal, charCode, or eval-based deobfuscation chains
rundll32.exe executing DLL from temp directory, Downloads, or user-writable path
rundll32.exe with command line referencing JavaScript, VBScript, or remote URL
rundll32.exe spawning child processes (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, net.exe)
rundll32.exe loading DLL not signed by Microsoft or known vendor
rundll32.exe making outbound network connections to external IP addresses
SMB connections to admin shares (C$, ADMIN$, IPC$) from unexpected source hosts
File copy operations over SMB to remote host followed by remote service or task creation
PsExec-style activity: named pipe creation on remote host following SMB session
EventID 5140 (network share accessed) for admin shares from non-admin workstation
Lateral movement pattern: authentication + share access + service creation on target
Email containing URL to credential harvesting page mimicking corporate login portal
User clicking link in email followed by credential submission to non-corporate domain
OAuth consent phishing: email link redirecting to OAuth authorization for malicious app
URL shortener or redirect chain in email body resolving to newly registered domain
Email link to cloud storage (OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox) hosting malicious payload
